frameworks.name: transcranial direct current stimulation (tDCS)
frameworks.one_line: anodal and cathodal polarities exert opposing modulation on cortical excitability

frameworks.name: GABA/glutamate neurochemical balance
frameworks.one_line: polarity-specific changes in inhibitory and excitatory transmitter synthesis explain excitability shifts

frameworks.name: visually evoked field potential read-out
frameworks.one_line: VEP amplitude indexes net neuronal excitability in cat area 21a

core_question: How does anodal versus cathodal tDCS alter cortical neuronal activity and the GABA/glutamate synthesis machinery in visual cortex?
key_assumptions: VEP amplitude tracks neuronal excitability in target cortex
tDCS effect localizes to stimulated region (area 21a)
transmitter synthesis enzymes (GAD65/67, GLS) index inhibitory and excitatory output
manipulations: post-stimulation time window for VEP measurement
biochemical assay of GABA and glutamate concentration and synthesizing enzymes
key_findings: anodal tDCS enhanced and cathodal tDCS suppressed VEP amplitude in area 21a
anodal tDCS selectively decreased GABA concentration and GAD65/GAD67 expression
cathodal tDCS selectively decreased glutamate concentration and glutaminase mRNA/protein
interpretations: polarity-specific tDCS effects arise from selective suppression of GABA versus glutamate synthesis
reduced inhibitory or excitatory transmitter output explains opposing excitability changes
